title: Dimitar and the Castle of Ice Fangsicle
summary: Dimitar, a small but experienced dragonet, is sent on a quest by the Mayor of his jungle town to find ice for the upcoming summer festival. His journey takes him to the highest mountains, where he encounters Ice Fangsicle, a massive, lonely dragon living in a castle made of ice. After Dimitar convinces the giant that he can find friendship among the dragonets, Ice Fangsicle joins him on the journey home. Together, they arrive just in time to celebrate the festival, where the giant dragon uses his icy breath to provide frozen treats for all the inhabitants.
audioFilename: Dimitar goes to Ginger school, and a white dragon story.m4a

highlightQuote.text: I've got a faster superhero thing... and my superhero cape keeps me at fly!

transcript.text: All right. Where do you want to be for stars and stories? On the floor?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: In your lap.
Oh, okay. Let me just sit over here.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Let's tell a Dimitar story.
A Dimitar story?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Dimitar! When you grow up to a bigger dra- to a dragon that's a bit bigger than you are now, you could go to Ginger school just like Seeker.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, now that's an interesting thought, Izzy kiddo, said Dimitar.
I'm the teacher at the school.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: I- I would love to visit this school, but I should- I should let you know that I'm quite old, actually. I'm even older than Seeker. I'm older than most grown-ups. Because we dragonets, we live a long time. I'm over 200 years old. So I'm not going to get any bigger. I'm my full size. I may look small, but I have a lot of years in me. And I know a lot of things. I've been a lot of places. But why don't you show me around this school? I'd be happy to go. Who's this Ginger you're talking about?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Of- I'm talking about the Ginger where she is perfectly... She made a very silly car made of basically Legos. But where is Ginger? Oh, I think I put her in the bed. Where is her? Fluff.
And Dimitar says, "Oh, is this Ginger?"
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: This is Ginger.
Well, very nice to meet you. You are very soft and fluffy and round, Ginger, I noticed.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, would you like to feel my tail, but pet it with your wing, Dimitar?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Sure, I'll give your tail a pet. Oh, it's such soft tail. I've never felt a tail so soft. Would you like to- to pet my tail? says Dimitar. Mine is rough and scaly, though. Oh, thanks for the pets. I haven't had a tail pet in a long time. Now, what's this school I heard about?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's- it's my school, says Ginger. There's planet maps you can work on.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Planet maps?

And wherever you see- and when you stop by a picture, that shows you which planet to do first and which planet to do next, so it's easy. And whenever you hear the music and stop at heart, that means it's time for circle when you're doing a planet map. Just to let you know, I'll show you.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: My goodness. Oh, thanks. Sounds like I have a lot to learn. Now let's start at the beginning. What is a planet, teacher?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: There's the planet map. It's as big as you can imagine that elephant. It's as big as that elephant over there.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: What's as big as the elephant?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The planet map's as big as the elephant.
Oh, it is. I see. And tell me about some of the planets on there.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: We got to turn on a flashlight to see in the dark. To see all the planets in the dark. Oh, okay. I see them now. Yes. Thank you. Do you see the planets?
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Now wait, good, good. Now what's- what's this one? And Dimitar pointed at a green planet.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Venus.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I see. And what's this one?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Saturn.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what about this one?
Jupiter.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: And what about this one?
Earth.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: That's Earth. Oh, I see. Is that where we are now?
Yes.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, okay. And how about this planet?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Mars.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: And this planet?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Uh, Europe.
Europe. Oh, okay. And what about this planet?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Uh, Australia.
Oh, I see. And then this one?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Moon.
The moon. Oh, okay, cool. And Dimitar said, "Wow, this planet map is very interesting. What else can you teach me about it?"
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Stop at heart! We stopped at heart. That means we have to do something. What do we have to do? Circle!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. How do we do circle?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We do circle by doing some story songs. So we do- every- we do- we do a story song in each little step. Oh, what's a story song? It's when you do like this: Once upon a time, there was a mouse named Hattie. She made clothing for everyone. If there was a friendly guy nearby... Can you repeat that song story part? Chapter one.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Chapter one, says Dimitar.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You got to sing that part like this: Once upon a time, there was a mouse named Hattie. Can you repeat that?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Once upon a time, there was a mouse named Hattie.
She sewed clothing for everyone, and she could make most anything with clothing that was all different colors and she could use soft fabric.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Can I do the next part? We'll take turns?
Yep. Did you hear what I- um, did you sing the part? Did you sing what I sang?
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: I didn't- I didn't remember all of what you sang because there was a lot of it. But I'll sing the next part. How about that?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: No, that's not the part you should be singing. You should be singing the right notes. Oh, well you sing a little bit. Sing a shorter part and I'll try to remember it and sing it back. She could make most clothes for anyone... and she...
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: She could make most clothes for anyone.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then...
And then...
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: And she could cut soft fabric.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: And she could cut soft fabric.
And she glued and let it and she could let it dry too. And then on- on another day, it would be dry and she'll hand it out.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: And she could glue it and let it dry. And on another day, it would be dry and she'll hand it out.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: To the person that it belonged to.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: To the person that it belonged to.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: When it was dry.
When it was dry.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Now we're gonna do a planet rhyming song. Okay? Mars and stars.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Mars and stars.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Moon and Mars.
Moon and Mars.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Earth and Sun.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Earth and Sun.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Sun and star.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Sun and star.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mars and Venus.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Mars and Venus.
Yay!
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Yeah. And then it's story time. Oh, it's story time, says Dimitar. What are we going to tell a story about?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Dimitar, you make up the story. Each kid takes a turn making up the story and then the teacher tells it back. That's how story time goes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. And Dimitar says, "Well, since you are asking about bigger dragons, and I am such a little one myself being a dragonet, perhaps you'd like to hear a story about a very big dragon that I met once, a long, long time ago."
What was its name?
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Well, let me start at the beginning. Once upon a time, I lived in a very warm part of the world in the jungle. And by the Mayor of that dragonet town, I was given a task. And the task was to bring back a good amount of ice for our summer festival to cool down our delicious drinks. And I took this task very seriously. And so I flew to where I thought it would be colder, up into the mountains. And the mountains, though they looked pretty close, were much further away than I thought. And it was a long, long journey and my wings became very tired. And as I flew, it did get colder and colder. And eventually I was high up in the mountains. And before long, I saw that the ground was covered with snow. And as I passed through some clouds higher and higher into the mountains, flying against the strong wind, it started to snow. And soon everything was white all around me. And I found that I had entered into a place that someone else lived. And I didn't know that they were there. But I could hear them. And they were making a very loud sound. It was "ROOOOAR! ROOOOAR!" And I said, "Oh, uh, hello? Who's there?" And the sound just kept going "ROOOOOAR!"
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Well Dimitar, then you must ring the doorbell.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Well, there was no door that I could see until I landed in the snow. And I noticed up ahead of me a building that was not like any ordinary building, but it was made all of huge blocks of ice. And I thought to myself, "Well, I'm on a mission to find some ice, so I must have come to the right place." And there was indeed a door and it did have a button next to it and I thought, "Well, maybe this is the doorbell." And so I pressed the button and it went "BLONG BLONG BLONG BLONG BLONG."
That's how some doorbells go different, Dimitar, so.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Well, yes, this one it sounded like it was a bell, maybe even a bell made of ice. And it was ringing quickly, "Blong blong blong blong blong," but it had a deep ringing sound. It must have been a very big bell. And this was a very, very big building made of ice. And as the snow cleared around it for a moment, I could see that it was actually a castle made of ice. And I thought, "Well, maybe whoever lives here will- will let me borrow some ice that I can bring home for the festival." And slowly the door swung open and I heard that sound again, "ROOOOOOAR!" And I said, "Um, excuse me." And the big voice said, "WHO COMES TO THE HOUSE OF ICE FANG-SICLE?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um, well, Dimitar was asking for some ice.

And Ice Fangsicle said, "Ice?" For the festival to keep the drinks cool. Well, there's no shortage of ice here. You must not be from around here. Here, watch this. And Ice Fangsicle, who was a giant ancient white dragon the size of a school bus, he dwarfed me. I had to look straight up into his face because he was hanging- standing above me. He said, "Watch this." And he puffed up his cheeks and he let out a big "WHHHHHOOOOOSH!" And the air from his breath suddenly froze into thousands of shards of ice that all suddenly fell to the ground with a clatter, "Brrr-clack!" And Dimitar said, "Wow, you can make ice with your breath." And Ice Fang said, "Yes, but I can't make friends." And Dimitar said, "Oh no, what do you mean?" And Ice Fangsicle said, "Yes, I live alone out here. I wish there were some other dragons that I could be friends with." And Dimitar said, "Well, I'm just here to gather some ice for- for the festival."
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Oh- oh, um, hey dragon, I've got some dragon friends! I've got some friends named- some- some great friends of yours! I've got some friends that you might want to test out and see a few!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, well, I thought maybe I was the only dragon in the world, but...
They're- they're called dragon- dragonets.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: What's a dragonet? said Ice Fangsicle. And Dimitar said, "Well, if you look at me, you might have to look closely." And the big white dragon brought his head down low to the ground to squint close at Dimitar, who was after all very tiny compared to him. He said, "Oh my goodness! Are you a baby? What kind of dragon are you?" And Dimitar said, "Nope, I'm a grown-up. I'm very old, actually, thank you very much. I'm a dragonet, which is a small kind of dragon. And if you'd help me carry this ice with me back where I live, where it is admittedly a bit warmer than here, uh, you could come visit our festival and perhaps make some friends there if you're willing to talk to us little dragonets." And Ice Fangsicle said, "Oh, thank you! And I'll make you all the ice that you want. We don't even have to bring this ice, ha ha!" And he gave it a kick and it swirled around. "I'll just blow you some more ice with my breath. We can do it as a party trick. Everyone bring their lemonade and I'll blow you some ice right into it. We'll make frozen drinks all day." And Dimitar said, "Wow, that's a wonderful idea. But it's a long trip back. Can I ride on your back?"
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Oh- oh, I can get your friend seeker! I know! I know! I- I have a superhero cape that can keep you warm while you fly. Oh, thank you, said Dimitar. And I've got a faster superhero thing! And my- and my superhero cape keeps me at fly! Wow, like this! And I do not get tired when I'm flying! Wow, you're such a fast flyer, said Dimitar. You can fly there on Dimitar and the other dragon! Get onto my back! Let's go for a ride!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so they all flew together back to the little town in the jungle where Dimitar lived, just in time for the summer festival. And they made everyone delicious frozen drinks to drink with the dragon's magic ice breath.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'll pretend this is the sun where Dimitar lives.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Ah, yes, the bright sun shone down on their summer festival and everyone had a good time sipping their frozen drinks. And that's the end of Stars and Stories.

characters.name: Dimitar
characters.description: A sharp-witted resident of a circular island castle, this two-century-old purple dragonet carries a pirate’s salt-crusted legacy and a fierce devotion to his salvaged library. Formerly of the Cadasori galleries, Seeker’s grumpy companion now roams the deep to save his ancestral shores, though a recent volcanic disturbance has left him uncharacteristically sickened and frail. Driven by habit and a tireless appetite for fish, he bickers incessantly over snacks while guarding his remaining volumes with a brooding, protective intensity.

characters.name: Ginger
characters.description: A soft, round, and silver-coated feline, this exceptionally fluffy educator is one of Minnie and Mickey’s own kittens grown into a figure of quiet authority. Known for daintily distributing napkins with velvet paws and commuting to school behind the wheel, the gray-furred teacher balances his role on Nana’s welcoming committee with a life of polite, helpful service. Whether presiding over his humble sanctuary—the very cage Seeker once coveted—or sustaining himself on crunchy kibble and savory wet food, he remains a steady presence, most recently seen trading his classroom duties for the shadows of a secret closet hideout alongside Garfield.

characters.name: Seeker
characters.description: A brave dwarven explorer and mediator, he resides in a glowing castle hub—a multiversal dock for skyships anchored within the skyline of New York City. From this urban sanctum, he conducts silver slime experiments and tends to wandering cats amidst a massive library. Frequently swapping between a red coat and a mystical purple cloak, his braided red beard remains his most eccentric tool, capable of catching small fish. A master of fusion, he combines ancient stone-craft with high-velocity mechanics, utilizing a legendary magic ladder to scale the shifting architecture of the realms. Whether brewing portal potions, harvesting floof-berries in Limbo with gemstone goggles, or aiding Buddy the Mouse, this walnut-loving student of the stars remains the hero who orchestrated the daring rescue of the world.

characters.name: Hattie the Mouse
characters.description: A nimble, friendly mouse of Possible Street and loyal friend to Izzy and Seeker, this brave artisan is the wife of Dimitar and mistress of the Workshop of Hats—a weathered hollow tree sanctuary on Cornell Street scented with cedar, ink, and peanut butter toast. Clad in her signature red cloak and pointy hat, she is a resourceful hatmaker whose spirit was forged against massive diggers and the rain-slicked Floofer Cloud. Within her paint-stained home, she carefully curates her husband’s library alongside her own hand-painted volumes while confronting a baffling supply mystery; the discovery of curious clover stickers signals a gathering shadow of intrigue she is determined to unravel.

characters.name: Icicle
characters.description: A titanic and ancient white ice dragon, this towering companion to Dimitar commands the skies with freezing breath and vast, powerful wings. From a shimmering ice palace atop a remote mountain peak, he presides over his crystalline domain with a timeless and frigid majesty, sustained exclusively by a diet of frozen fish.

characters.name: Mayor of Dragonet Town
characters.description: The leader of Dimitar's jungle town who tasks him with finding ice.

places.name: Ginger School
places.description: A school where students learn about planets and sing story songs.

places.name: Ice Castle
places.description: Perched high in the snowy peaks, Icecycle’s lonely fortress is a majestic bastion of solitude constructed from massive blocks of ice. Its jagged, frozen surfaces are meticulously buffed with specialized polishing kits, transforming the mountaintop stronghold into a brilliant, diamond-like spectacle.

places.name: Dragonet Town
places.description: A warm town located in the jungle where Dimitar and other dragonets live.
